POS ON JOINT P & A IN REPLY TO RESP.'S OPPOS. MEMO; REPLICATION TO ANSWER FILED BY PETITIONER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION INFORMATION CENTER 'EPIC' A NON-PROFIT CALIFORNIA CORPORATION SIERRA CLUB, A NON-PROFIT CALIFORNIA CALIFORNIA TROUT INC., A NON PROFIT CALIFORNIA CORPORATION (PETITIONER FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE #508127)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION INFORMATION CENTER, SIERRA CLUB, AND CALIFORNIA TROUT, INC. JOINT MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE CONSOLIDATED PETITIONS FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED. PETITIONER'S CLAIMS ARE RIPE FOR ADJUDICATION. PETITIONERS PRESENT A PURE LEGAL ISSUE (FACIAL CHALLENGE) THAT IS NOT DEPENDENT UPON A SET OF HYPOTHETICAL FACTS. RESPONDENTS ADOPTED REGULATIONS IN EXCESS OF THEIR STATUTORY AUTHORITIES. RESPONDENT DFG'S "INCIDENTAL TAKE PERMIT GUIDELINES FOR TIMBER OPERATIONS 2007" SECTIONS 787.1(A)(1), 787.2, 787.4(A), 787.5(A) & (B), 787.6(A), AND 787.9 VIOLATE THE FAIR ARGUMENT STANDARD. ITP GUIDELINE 787.9(D) SUFFERS FROM THE SAME INFIRMITY THAT WAS IDENTIFIED IN COMMUNITIES FOR A BETTER ENVIRONMENT VS. CALIFORNIA RESOURCES AGENCY (2002) 103 CAL.APP. 4TH 98. THE REGULATION ALSO VIOLATES CEQA'S MANDATORY FINDINGS OF SIGNIFICANCE AND THE CESA DUTY TO CONSIDER JEOPARDY. RESPONDENT BOF'S "COHO SALMON INCIDENTAL TAKE ASSISTANCE 2007" EXCEEDS THE AUTHORITY OF BOF BY ADOPTING REGULATIONS THAT PURPORT TO DETERMINE MINIMIZATION AND FULL MITIGATION UNDER CESA (FISH & GAMES CODE SECTION 2081), WHERE BOF HAS NO AUTHORITY TO MAKE A DETERMINATION OF WHAT CONSTITUTES MINIMIZATION AND FULL MITIGATION UNDER CESA. ACCORDINGLY, SECTIONS 916.9.2 [936.9.2], AND 923.9.2 [943.9.2] ARE STRUCK DOWN. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS THE COMMENT ISSUE. THIS ACTION IS NOT TIME-BARRED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR #12365

calendar.matters:
Joint Motion For Judgment On The Consolidated Petitions For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION INFORMATION CENTER, Sierra Club, and California Trout, Inc. Joint Motion for Judgment on the Consolidated Petitions for Writ of Mandate.
Sharon E. Duggan, Esq. Law Offices of Sharon E. Duggan, (510) 271-0825, appearing for Petitioners/Plaintiffs, & Jason R. Flanders, of Kenyon/Yeats LLP (916) 609-5000, appearing for Petitioners/Plaintiffs; Kevin Lesh, Esq. Deputy Attorney Gen. State of Cal., (415) 703-5607, appearing for respondents/Defendants and moving party; William Sloan, Esq. of Morrison Foerster (415) 268-6841, appearing for Intervenors. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Petitioner's claims are ripe for adjudication. Petitioners present a pure legal issue (facial challenge) that is not dependent upon a set of hypothetical facts. Respondents adopted regulations in excess of their statutory authorities. Respondent DFG's "Incidental Take Permit Guidelines for Timber Operations 2007" sections 787.1(a)(1), 787.2, 787.4(a), 787.5(a) & (b), 787.6(a), and 787.9 violate the fair argument standard. ITP Guideline 787.9(d) suffers from the same infirmity that was identified in Communities for a Better Environment vs. California Resources Agency (2002) 103 Cal.App. 4th 98. The regulation also violates CEQA's Mandatory Findings of Significance and the CESA duty to consider jeopardy. Respondent BOF's "Coho Salmon Incidental Take Assistance 2007" exceeds the authority of BOF by adopting regulations that purport to determine minimization and full mitigation under CESA (Fish & Games Code section 2081), where BOF has no authority to make a determination of what constitutes minimization and full mitigation under CESA. Accordingly, sections 916.9.2 [936.9.2], and 923.9.2 [943.9.2] are struck down. The Court does not address the comment issue. This action is not time-barred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR

APPEARANCES: SHARON E. DUGGAN, ESQUIRE (510.271.0825) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ROBERT W.
